subPoints: Good use of Pandas
Plotting - good, concise coding for the plot generation
Plotting - well formatted plots (e.g. axis labels, fontsize, color)
Delimiter (i.e., `sep`) - incorrectly specified the separator used within the CSV-formatted file
Header (i.e., `header`) - incorrectly specified the header line
Incorrectly read in the data
you did not clean the data properly (e.g., `drop_duplicates`,  `dropna` or `drop`
you did not save the cleaned results (e.g., using `inplace` or assigning it to a new object)
The data was not plotted using the Pandas' library only
The data should plotted should have all the same units (i.e. kJ/mol)
The plots do not have proper axes labels
The axes labels do not include units
The plot does not include a legend
The fontsize in the plot could have been better
Not saved - the final data was not saved
Some of the required data was not saved

negative.mainPoint: Coding concepts that were poorly done, which includes the following

built-in function - 'index' for lists
use of an iterable container (e.g., list, tuple) to store data (e.g., energies, distances)

The numerical solutions were wrong.
The solutions were not given with the correct units.
The lowest energy value was incorrectly identified. It should have been -1.6e-21 at a distance of 3.8 Å.
